title: Planning a Wedding
summary: A bride meets with a florist to discuss flower arrangements and decor for her upcoming wedding.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Hi, I'm Sophie. I called last week about flowers for my wedding in September.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: Hello, Sophie! I'm Claire. Please come in and sit down. Tell me a little about what you have in mind.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Well, the venue is a country house with a large garden. We're having about eighty guests, and the ceremony will be outdoors.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: That sounds lovely. An outdoor ceremony gives us so many options. Do you have a colour palette in mind?

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: I'm thinking soft whites and pale pinks, with some greenery. I want it to feel natural and relaxed, not too formal.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: Perfect. That style is very popular right now. We could use garden roses, peonies, and eucalyptus for a soft, romantic look.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Oh, I love peonies! Would they be available in September?

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: Peonies peak in late spring, but we can often source them through specialist suppliers for autumn weddings. I would recommend confirming availability by June.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Good to know. What about the bridal bouquet? I want something I can actually carry comfortably, not too heavy.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: A loose, garden-style bouquet would suit you well. We would use a mix of roses, peonies, and trailing greenery. It feels effortless and moves beautifully as you walk.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: That sounds exactly right. And we also need flowers for the tables. We'll have ten round tables.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: For round tables, low centrepieces tend to work best so guests can see each other across the table. We could use low glass vases filled with loose blooms.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: I like that idea. Would the centrepieces match the bouquet?

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: Yes, we always aim for a cohesive look throughout the whole event. The same palette and textures will tie everything together.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Wonderful. What about the arch for the ceremony? My partner and I will exchange our vows beneath it.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: A floral arch is one of my favourite things to create. We can dress it heavily with cascading flowers and foliage, or keep it lighter and more airy. Which do you prefer?

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: I think lighter and airy suits the garden setting better. I don't want it to look too overwhelming.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: I completely agree. A lighter arch lets the natural surroundings breathe and keeps the focus on the two of you.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: That's a lovely way to put it. Can you give me a rough idea of the total cost for everything we've discussed?

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: For the bouquet, ten centrepieces, and the arch, you're looking at roughly fifteen hundred to two thousand pounds, depending on the final flower choices and the arch size.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: That's within my budget. I'm really happy with how this has gone. When do you need a deposit to secure the date?

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: A thirty percent deposit within two weeks would secure your date. I'll send you a written quote by email tomorrow, and we can make any adjustments before you confirm.

body.speaker: Bride
body.text: Perfect. Thank you so much, Claire. I feel much more confident about the flowers now.

body.speaker: Florist
body.text: It's my pleasure, Sophie. Your wedding sounds absolutely beautiful. I can't wait to bring it to life.

annotations.meaningVi: bảng màu, tông màu chủ đạo
annotations.exampleEn: The designer chose a colour palette of creams and golds for the reception.

annotations.meaningVi: cây lá xanh, tán lá trang trí
annotations.exampleEn: The florist added greenery to the bouquet to give it a natural feel.

annotations.meaningVi: hoa hồng vườn (loại hoa hồng có cánh dày đặc)
annotations.exampleEn: Garden roses are popular at weddings because of their full, soft shape.
Peonies are one of the most popular flowers for bridal bouquets.

annotations.meaningVi: tìm kiếm và cung cấp, lấy nguồn hàng
annotations.exampleEn: We can source rare flowers through specialist growers.
The restaurant sources its ingredients from specialist suppliers.
She chose a white and pink bridal bouquet with trailing ivy.
Trailing greenery adds an elegant, natural touch to floral arrangements.

annotations.meaningVi: hoa trang trí trung tâm bàn tiệc
annotations.exampleEn: The centrepieces on the tables were tall vases of white lilies.

annotations.meaningVi: thống nhất, gắn kết, đồng bộ
annotations.exampleEn: A cohesive colour scheme makes the whole event look professional.

annotations.meaningVi: kết nối mọi thứ lại với nhau, tạo sự thống nhất
annotations.exampleEn: The gold ribbon tied everything together in the room design.
The arch was decorated with cascading flowers and ribbons.
The decorator used a mix of flowers and foliage for the table arrangements.

annotations.meaningVi: đặt chỗ giữ ngày, xác nhận ngày
annotations.exampleEn: You need to pay a deposit to secure the date with the venue.
Please send us a written quote before we confirm the order.

grammarNotes.title: Conditional Sentences — Type 1 (Real Conditions)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Hội thoại sử dụng câu điều kiện loại 1 để diễn đạt các tình huống thực tế: 'If peonies are not available, we can use garden roses instead' (hàm ý). Câu điều kiện loại 1 dùng 'if + simple present' ở mệnh đề điều kiện và 'will/can + verb' ở mệnh đề kết quả. Trong ngữ cảnh thực tế như lên kế hoạch đám cưới, loại câu này rất phổ biến khi nói về các tình huống có thể xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The dialogue uses first conditional patterns to discuss real possibilities: 'We can often source them through specialist suppliers' implies conditions and outcomes. First conditional uses 'if + simple present' in the condition clause and 'will/can + verb' in the result clause. In practical planning conversations, these structures are very common when discussing likely scenarios.

grammarNotes.title: Polite Suggestions with 'could' and 'would'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong hội thoại lịch sự về dịch vụ, 'could' và 'would' được dùng để đưa ra đề xuất và đề nghị một cách nhẹ nhàng: 'We could use garden roses', 'A loose bouquet would suit you well', 'We would use a mix of roses'. Dùng 'could' thay 'can' và 'would' thay 'will' làm giọng điệu mềm mỏng và chuyên nghiệp hơn, đặc biệt trong bối cảnh dịch vụ khách hàng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: In polite service conversations, 'could' and 'would' soften suggestions and recommendations: 'We could use garden roses', 'A loose bouquet would suit you well', 'We would use a mix of roses'. Using 'could' instead of 'can' and 'would' instead of 'will' creates a softer, more professional tone — especially appropriate in customer service contexts.

translationVi:
Cô dâu: Xin chào, tôi là Sophie. Tôi đã gọi điện tuần trước về hoa cho đám cưới của tôi vào tháng Chín.

Thợ cắm hoa: Xin chào, Sophie! Tôi là Claire. Mời vào và ngồi xuống. Hãy kể cho tôi nghe một chút về những gì bạn đang nghĩ đến.

Cô dâu: Vâng, địa điểm là một ngôi nhà ở vùng quê với một khu vườn lớn. Chúng tôi có khoảng tám mươi khách mời, và lễ cưới sẽ được tổ chức ngoài trời.

Thợ cắm hoa: Nghe thật tuyệt vời. Lễ cưới ngoài trời cho chúng ta rất nhiều lựa chọn. Bạn có bảng màu nào trong đầu chưa?

Cô dâu: Tôi đang nghĩ đến màu trắng nhẹ và hồng nhạt, với một ít lá xanh. Tôi muốn không khí tự nhiên và thoải mái, không quá trang trọng.

Thợ cắm hoa: Hoàn hảo. Phong cách đó rất phổ biến hiện nay. Chúng ta có thể dùng hoa hồng vườn, mẫu đơn và bạch đàn để tạo vẻ nhẹ nhàng, lãng mạn.

Cô dâu: Ôi, tôi rất thích hoa mẫu đơn! Chúng có sẵn vào tháng Chín không?

Thợ cắm hoa: Mẫu đơn nở rộ vào cuối mùa xuân, nhưng chúng ta thường có thể đặt hàng từ các nhà cung cấp chuyên biệt cho các đám cưới mùa thu. Tôi khuyên bạn nên xác nhận tình trạng hàng trước tháng Sáu.

Cô dâu: Cảm ơn đã cho biết. Còn bó hoa cô dâu thì sao? Tôi muốn bó hoa mà tôi có thể cầm thoải mái, không quá nặng.

Thợ cắm hoa: Một bó hoa kiểu vườn, nhẹ nhàng sẽ rất phù hợp với bạn. Chúng ta sẽ kết hợp hoa hồng, mẫu đơn và dây lá rủ. Trông sẽ rất tự nhiên và đẹp khi bạn bước đi.

Cô dâu: Nghe đúng như những gì tôi muốn. Và chúng tôi cũng cần hoa trang trí bàn tiệc. Chúng tôi có mười bàn tròn.

Thợ cắm hoa: Đối với bàn tròn, hoa trang trí thấp thường hiệu quả nhất để khách có thể nhìn thấy nhau qua bàn. Chúng ta có thể dùng bình thủy tinh thấp đựng đầy hoa.

Cô dâu: Tôi thích ý tưởng đó. Hoa trang trí bàn có hợp với bó hoa không?

Thợ cắm hoa: Có, chúng tôi luôn hướng đến vẻ nhất quán trong toàn bộ sự kiện. Cùng một bảng màu và kết cấu sẽ gắn kết mọi thứ lại.

Cô dâu: Tuyệt vời. Còn vòm hoa cho buổi lễ thì sao? Tôi và người bạn đời sẽ trao lời thề dưới đó.

Thợ cắm hoa: Vòm hoa là một trong những thứ tôi yêu thích nhất khi tạo ra. Chúng ta có thể trang trí dày đặc với hoa và lá rủ xuống, hoặc giữ nhẹ nhàng và thoáng hơn. Bạn thích kiểu nào?

Cô dâu: Tôi nghĩ nhẹ nhàng và thoáng sẽ phù hợp hơn với không gian vườn. Tôi không muốn trông quá cầu kỳ.

Thợ cắm hoa: Tôi hoàn toàn đồng ý. Vòm nhẹ nhàng để không gian tự nhiên xung quanh thở và giữ sự chú ý vào hai bạn.

Cô dâu: Nói thật hay lắm. Bạn có thể cho tôi biết sơ qua tổng chi phí cho những gì chúng ta đã thảo luận không?

Thợ cắm hoa: Với bó hoa, mười hoa trang trí bàn và vòm hoa, bạn cần khoảng từ một nghìn năm trăm đến hai nghìn bảng Anh, tùy thuộc vào lựa chọn hoa cuối cùng và kích thước vòm.

Cô dâu: Điều đó nằm trong ngân sách của tôi. Tôi rất vui về cách buổi tư vấn này diễn ra. Khi nào bạn cần tiền đặt cọc để giữ ngày?

Thợ cắm hoa: Đặt cọc ba mươi phần trăm trong vòng hai tuần sẽ giữ ngày cho bạn. Tôi sẽ gửi báo giá bằng email vào ngày mai, và chúng ta có thể điều chỉnh trước khi bạn xác nhận.

Cô dâu: Hoàn hảo. Cảm ơn rất nhiều, Claire. Tôi cảm thấy tự tin hơn nhiều về hoa rồi.

Thợ cắm hoa: Rất vui được giúp bạn, Sophie. Đám cưới của bạn nghe thật tuyệt đẹp. Tôi rất mong được hiện thực hóa nó.

questions.prompt: Where will Sophie's wedding ceremony take place?
questions.explanation: Sophie says 'the venue is a country house with a large garden' and 'the ceremony will be outdoors.'
questions.hint: Sophie describes the venue and location of the ceremony.

questions.prompt: What colour palette does Sophie want for her wedding?
options: Soft whites and pale pinks with greenery
Gold and ivory with no foliage
Blue and white in a nautical theme
questions.explanation: Sophie says 'I'm thinking soft whites and pale pinks, with some greenery.'
questions.hint: Sophie describes the colours she has in mind.

questions.prompt: Why does Claire recommend confirming peony availability by June?
options: Because peonies are always unavailable in September
Because peonies peak in late spring and may need to be sourced from specialist suppliers
Because June is when the florist takes her summer holiday
Because prices for peonies rise sharply after June
questions.explanation: Claire explains 'Peonies peak in late spring, but we can often source them through specialist suppliers for autumn weddings. I would recommend confirming availability by June.'
questions.hint: Claire gives a specific reason related to the flowering season.

questions.prompt: What type of bridal bouquet does Claire suggest for Sophie?
options: A tight, structured bouquet of white lilies
A loose, garden-style bouquet with roses, peonies, and trailing greenery
A large, heavy bunch of sunflowers
questions.explanation: Claire suggests 'A loose, garden-style bouquet' with 'a mix of roses, peonies, and trailing greenery.'
questions.hint: Claire describes the bouquet style that will match Sophie's relaxed theme.

questions.prompt: Why does Claire recommend low centrepieces for the round tables?
options: Because low centrepieces are less expensive
Because they are easier to transport
So that guests can see each other across the table
Because tall arrangements are not allowed at the venue
questions.explanation: Claire says 'low centrepieces tend to work best so guests can see each other across the table.'
questions.hint: Claire gives a practical reason related to guest comfort.

questions.prompt: How does Claire describe the purpose of using the same palette throughout the event?
options: It reduces the total cost of flowers
It ties everything together for a cohesive look
It is required by the venue
questions.explanation: Claire says 'the same palette and textures will tie everything together' and that she always aims for 'a cohesive look throughout the whole event.'
questions.hint: Claire explains the visual reason for consistency.

questions.prompt: What will Sophie and her partner do beneath the floral arch?
questions.explanation: Sophie says 'My partner and I will exchange our vows beneath it.'
questions.hint: Sophie explains why the arch is important.

questions.prompt: Why does Sophie prefer a lighter arch over a heavily decorated one?
options: She does not want it to look too overwhelming
The venue rules do not allow heavy decorations
questions.explanation: Sophie says 'I don't want it to look too overwhelming.'
questions.hint: Sophie gives her personal aesthetic reason.

questions.prompt: What is the approximate total cost Claire quotes for the main flower arrangements?
options: Five hundred to eight hundred pounds
One thousand to one thousand two hundred pounds
Fifteen hundred to two thousand pounds
Two thousand five hundred to three thousand pounds
questions.explanation: Claire says 'you're looking at roughly fifteen hundred to two thousand pounds.'
questions.hint: Claire gives a price range for the bouquet, centrepieces, and arch.

questions.prompt: What does Claire say she will send Sophie the next day?
questions.explanation: Claire says 'I'll send you a written quote by email tomorrow.'
questions.hint: Claire describes her next step after the meeting.

questions.prompt: How much deposit does Claire require to secure the wedding date?
questions.explanation: Claire says 'A thirty percent deposit within two weeks would secure your date.'

questions.prompt: How does Sophie feel at the end of the consultation?
options: Much more confident about the flowers
questions.explanation: Sophie says 'I feel much more confident about the flowers now.'
questions.hint: Sophie gives a direct statement of how she feels.

cloze.template:
Sophie: Hi, I'm Sophie. I called last week about flowers for my wedding in {{b1}}.

Claire: Hello, Sophie! Please come in and sit down. Tell me a little about what you have in mind.

Sophie: The venue is a country house with a large garden. We're having about eighty {{b2}}, and the ceremony will be outdoors.

Claire: That sounds lovely. Do you have a colour {{b3}} in mind?

Sophie: I'm thinking soft whites and pale pinks, with some {{b4}}. I want it to feel natural and relaxed, not too formal.

Claire: Perfect. We could use garden roses, peonies, and eucalyptus for a soft, {{b5}} look.

Sophie: Would peonies be available in September?

Claire: Peonies peak in late spring, but we can often {{b6}} them through specialist suppliers. I would recommend confirming availability by June.

Sophie: Good to know. What about the bridal {{b7}}? I want something I can carry comfortably, not too heavy.

Claire: A loose, garden-style bouquet would suit you well. It feels effortless and moves beautifully as you walk.

Sophie: We also need flowers for the tables. We'll have ten round tables.

Claire: For round tables, low {{b8}} tend to work best so guests can see each other across the table.

Sophie: What about the arch? My partner and I will exchange our {{b9}} beneath it.

Claire: We can dress it heavily with cascading flowers and foliage, or keep it lighter and more airy.

Sophie: I think lighter suits the garden setting better. I don't want it to look too {{b10}}.

Claire: A lighter arch lets the natural surroundings breathe and keeps the focus on the two of you.

blanks.explanation: Sophie says her wedding is 'in September' — this is stated in her opening line.
'Guests' is the standard word for people attending a wedding or event — 'about eighty guests'.
'Colour palette' is the specific phrase used — it means the set of colours chosen for a design or event.
'Greenery' is the word Sophie uses — it refers to decorative leaves and plant stems used alongside flowers.
Claire describes the look as 'soft, romantic' — matching Sophie's request for a natural, relaxed feel.
'Source' means to find and obtain a supply of something — 'we can often source them through specialist suppliers'.
'Bridal bouquet' is the specific term for the bunch of flowers a bride carries — not simply 'flowers' or 'bunch'.
'Centrepieces' are floral arrangements placed at the centre of dining tables — the specific term used by Claire.
'Exchange vows' is the fixed phrase for the moment of making marriage promises — 'exchange our vows beneath it'.
Sophie says she doesn't want the arch to look 'too overwhelming' — meaning too much, too dominant, or overpowering.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sophie wants her wedding flowers to feel 'natural and relaxed, not too formal'. How do the choices people make about their wedding reflect their personalities and values? Do you think the way people celebrate important events has changed in recent years, and if so, why?
